title: Sabo Defies Orders
description: Pages 6–10 focus on sabo defies orders, keeping the Dressrosa conflict centered on the active characters in this part.
scene: This part follows Unknown person #1, Unknown person #2, Unknown person #3, Unknown person #4, Unknown person #5, Trebol, Unknown person #6 as sabo abandons restraint to protect Luffy, clashing with Fujitora as the final Dressrosa battle gathers everyone around the palace.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Stooooop!!! Have you forgotten how I used to take care of you?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: peh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh he

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: ...!! Do you remember the one thing that I hate the most?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #18
dialogue.text: for a bunch of lowly scum like you...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #19
dialogue.text: To be able to rise up and think for even an instant that you had a chance of winning is a disgrace I cannot bear!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #20
dialogue.text: For I... I am of the world's greatest and most noble bloodlines ....!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #22
dialogue.text: Greatness was not only my birthright, but I also acquired one of the rarest abilities in this world!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #25
dialogue.text: But....!!! that prestige and stature that rightfully belonged to me...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #26
dialogue.text: One day, my father abandoned all of it...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #27
dialogue.text: And our family of four was brought down into this garbage dump of a world!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #29
dialogue.text: Spouting crap like we need to "live like real humans"...?! He was such a stupid, foolish father....!!

summary: Sabo Defies Orders develops the chapter's Dressrosa conflict through Unknown person #1, Unknown person #2, Unknown person #3, Unknown person #4, Unknown person #5, while preserving the OCR dialogue and keeping unclear speakers readable.
